comment: Criteria registry (TDD §14, guardrail 18). §14 must match this registry (comparison test); the traceability matrix below maps each criterion to named live tests or QA-record fields, and the bidirectional meta-test closes both directions over this registry, itself included. Applicability: a standalone edition release requires AC-01–AC-19; bundle creation additionally requires AC-20.

criteria.text: Source extraction reproduces the edition's normative census exactly (§3), including the authored dependency map cross-validated against parsed claim references; PCT claims 1–18, Examples 1–5, Tables 1–3 with row anchors, four figures present and hash-verified.
Every owner carries its applicable lifecycle fields with a valid review.contentHash over the review projection (incl. identity tuples and dependency-chain hash); zero pending, zero stale.

Gate referential integrity passes in both directions with declared uniqueness keys; every mandatory gate has a reviewed disposition; the no-candidate disposition fixture releases without a fabricated mapping; the inventory-completeness human attestation is current.
Zero unresolved source drift: all corpus, fragment, aggregate-claim, dependency-chain, target, and caution-source digests current (full digests, matching canonVersion).

All schema and invariant validations pass: axis tags present with inter-axis invariants and declared locator exceptions satisfied; applicability matrices; relation-binding conformity; phrase rules; no duplicate targets, gate instances, or dispositions; note and rationale bounds; caution type×scope matrix and code closure; caution sources resolve into quotable blocks only; zero targets on editorial, excluded, or internal blocks; enum ↔ presentation coverage; forbidden-terms check clean.
The edition's fixtures (including the disposition fixture) validate against schemas and pinned corpora; this document's examples match the fixture projections.

Registry-access, edition-blindness, writer-set, diff-classifier, single-digest-path, and procedure-vs-matrix tests pass; the migration case-table scenario test passes against a mutated corpus copy; canonical serialization golden vectors pass; property-based canonicalization tests (idempotence, NFC duplicate-key rejection, escaping exactness, integer edge rules over generated inputs) pass.
Projection tests pass: no never-tagged field and no internal QA-source identifier appears in any artifact kind, previews included.
Adversarial escaping fixtures render safely through every authored and quoted channel.
Forward and reverse navigation behave per §§5–6, including claim-level gate display (scripted checks on the built candidate).
Keyboard-only operation, focus determinism, and screen-reader announcements per §12 against the release-approved support matrix (manual, against candidate bytes, recorded in the QA record).
Print output per §12 (manual, against candidate bytes).
Local-file operation across the support matrix (schema/support-matrix.json), including minimum-viewport and stacked layouts.
With JavaScript disabled, the §11 static content is fully readable.
Attempted-use instrumentation per schema/api-policy.json records zero external requests, zero cookie writes, zero Web Storage/IndexedDB use, and zero navigation-API mutation attempts.

Double build byte-identical across separate interpreter processes; content-input lock reproduces byte-identically between candidate and release (diagnostics excluded); exact-set check passes in both directions; release byte-compares against the QA'd candidate and seals the same bytes; lock, envelope, and release-record written append-only; checksums emitted and matching.
§9.1 disclaimer and approved legend present on screen, in print, and no-JS; legend approval matches the shipped wording digest.
Double-sided QA attestations current, authored via the typed commands: priority-map cross-check and, for AF, the crosswalk non-conflation check.

The traceability meta-test passes over the criteria registry: every registered criterion (AC-01 through AC-20, including this one) maps to named live tests or QA-record fields, and every acceptance-designated test maps back to a registered criterion.

The bundle matches its config exactly (enumerated members only, including the bundle-manifest), every member digest verified, deterministic STORE ZIP conforming to the golden bundle fixture with its own detached checksum, and the neutral manifest matching its approved wording digest.
